county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1824; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); North Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives (Tallahassee) and Alachua County Clerk of Circuit Court (Gainesville). Home to the University of Florida and the P.K. Yonge Library of Florida History, which holds extensive Spanish colonial records and territorial-era documents. Alachua was formed 1824 during the territorial period — check St. Johns and Duval for earlier records. Seminole Wars displaced many early settlers; check military records at NARA for context on frontier settlement patterns. Matheson History Museum has local genealogical resources. Alachua County had a significant enslaved population on cotton and sugar plantations.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books for Alachua County from 1824 forward; microfilm copies at Florida State Archives

source_profiles.survival_notes: County marriage records from formation; statewide recording began 1927

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1861; antebellum county; North Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Baker County Clerk (Macclenny). Formed 1861 from New River County (renamed Bradford 1861). Small rural county in northeast Florida. Pre-1861 records in Columbia and New River (Bradford). Olustee Battlefield (1864) — largest Civil War battle in Florida — generates military records. Check Florida State Archives for territorial tax rolls. Baker County Historical Society has local materials. Many families also appear in Nassau and Duval records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1913; 20th-century formation; Panhandle region
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Bay County Clerk (Panama City). Formed 1913 from Washington and Calhoun — a relatively young county. Pre-1913 records split between parent counties. Panama City developed as a port and timber town. Tyndall Air Force Base generates 20th-century military records. Bay County Public Library has local history collections. Hurricane Michael (2018) damaged some records; the courthouse survived but check for any gaps. St. Andrew Bay area settlement records trace to 1820s fishing communities.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1858; antebellum county; North Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Bradford County Clerk (Starke). Originally named New River County (1858), renamed Bradford 1861. Pre-1858 records in Columbia. Camp Blanding (WWII) records at NARA and Florida State Archives. Bradford County Historical Society has local materials. Strawberry farming heritage. Cross-reference with Baker County, which was carved from New River the same year. Santa Fe River area settlement patterns.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1855; antebellum county; Central Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Brevard County Clerk (Titusville). Originally part of Mosquito County (renamed Orange 1845) and old St. Lucie County. Indian River area pioneer families. Kennedy Space Center (Cape Canaveral) transformed the county in the 1950s-60s. Brevard County Historical Commission and North Brevard Historical Museum have local records. Fishing and citrus industry records. The county was very sparsely populated before the 20th century.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1915; 20th-century formation; South Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Broward County Clerk (Fort Lauderdale). Formed 1915 from Dade (now Miami-Dade) and Palm Beach — one of Florida's youngest counties. Pre-1915 records in parent counties. Seminole Tribe of Florida has presence in Broward (Big Cypress area). Everglades drainage projects (1905+) shaped settlement. Fort Lauderdale Historical Society has local collections. Rapid population growth began in the 1920s Florida land boom. New River Settlement records at Florida State Archives date to the 1830s.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1838; formed during territorial period (pre-statehood); Panhandle region
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Calhoun County Clerk (Blountstown). Formed 1838 from Jackson and Franklin during the territorial period. Apalachicola River area. Pre-1838 records in Jackson. Small rural county in the Florida Panhandle. Blountstown is named for Seminole chief John Blount. Calhoun County Public Library has limited local history. Check Apalachicola land records at Florida State Archives. Creek and Seminole history relevant to the area.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1921; 20th-century formation; South Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Charlotte County Clerk (Punta Gorda). Formed 1921 from DeSoto — a 20th-century county. Pre-1921 records in DeSoto (and earlier in Manatee). Peace River area settlement. Charlotte Harbor was a significant fishing and trading area since the Spanish period. Charlotte County Historical Center has local materials. The 1910s phosphate boom attracted settlers. Hurricane Charley (2004) damaged some local records — verify status with clerk.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1887; post-Civil War formation; Central Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Citrus County Clerk (Inverness). Formed 1887 from Hernando. Pre-1887 records in Hernando (and earlier in Alachua). Crystal River area has deep archaeological history. Citrus County Historical Society has local collections. Named for the citrus industry that dominated the area. Coastal Heritage Museum in Crystal River has pioneer-era materials. Withlacoochee State Forest area — check state land records at Florida State Archives.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Florida county formed 1858; antebellum county; North Florida
county_metadata.research_tips: Florida State Archives and Clay County Clerk (Green Cove Springs). Formed 1858 from Duval. Pre-1858 records in Duval. Camp Blanding Military Reservation (shared with Bradford) generates military records. Green Cove Springs was a popular 19th-century resort. Clay County Archives and Historical Society has local materials. Middleburg area families predate county formation. Black Creek and St. Johns River area settlement records at Florida State Archives.
